joins: LEFT JOIN recent_customers r ON h.customer_id = r.customer_id
filters: order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 1095) ), historical_customers AS ( SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM customer_transactions_table WHERE order_date < DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 1095) ) SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT h.customer_id) AS lapse_customers FROM historical_customers h LEFT JOIN recent_customers r ON h.customer_id = r.customer_id WHERE r.customer_id IS NULL;
date_filters: order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 1095) ), historical_customers AS ( SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM customer_transactions_table WHERE order_date < DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), 1095) ) SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT h.customer_id) AS lapse_customers FROM historical_customers h LEFT JOIN recent_customers r ON h.customer_id = r.customer_id WHERE r.customer_id IS NULL;
parser_limitations: This parser is intentionally lightweight and works best for common analytical SQL.
Complex nested queries, CTEs, vendor-specific syntax, and deeply nested expressions may require manual review.
risk_flags: Query includes joins. Confirm join keys and join type to avoid duplicate or unmatched records.
